subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF ECF/Art IV, World Bank, V-Dem, Freedom House, RSF, BTI) + international press (Reuters, AFP, France 24, Al Jazeera) and Africa Center/ICG on the constitutional change and Sahel spillover.

A 58-year family dynasty entrenched: a 2024-25 shift to a parliamentary system let Faure Gnassingbe become President of the Council of Ministers - indefinitely renewable, criticized as a 'constitutional coup' - met by 2025 protests and a deadly crackdown; steady ~5.3% growth and an on-track IMF program; the WAEMU CFA franc is pegged to the euro.

Standout features: an indefinitely entrenched Gnassingbe dynasty via the 2024 'constitutional coup'; the strategic Lome deep-water port and phosphate base; and rising-but-still-contained jihadist spillover into the northern Savanes region.

economic.notes: Steady ~5.3% growth, low peg-anchored inflation, and an on-track IMF program, with elevated debt and a still-narrow base the constraints.

fiscal_state.notes: Consolidating under a 2024 IMF ECF (first review passed) from a high debt level (~67-70% of GDP), targeting the WAEMU 3% deficit norm. (IMF Dec-2024)

monetary_independence.notes: The CFA-franc euro peg delivers credibility but zero autonomy under a France-guaranteed, BCEAO-pooled regime. (BCEAO/IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The CFA franc is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Low and peg-anchored (~2-3%) - a structural strength. (IMF)

banking_stability.notes: Stable within WAEMU, with sovereign-exposure concentration the main watch-item. (IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: The Lome deep-water port is a regional transhipment hub and phosphate a key export, with a local-processing push; offset by a narrow commodity base. (IMF; World Bank)

institutional.notes: Usable macro/statistical capacity, but rule of law and the press are squeezed by entrenched single-family rule.

rule_of_law.notes: Weak and politically subordinate - a 2024-25 constitutional rewrite re-engineered the executive to entrench Gnassingbe, with courts deferential. (V-Dem; Freedom House; BTI)

statistical_integrity.notes: Usable and IMF-engaged through program reviews. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Moderate, with patronage and capacity gaps. (World Bank/BTI)

press_freedom.notes: Declining - RSF 121/180 in 2025, with outlet suspensions (Liberte, L'Alternative) and state-advertising leverage driving self-censorship. (RSF)

political_social.notes: A 58-year dynasty entrenched indefinitely by the 2024 reform, met by 2025 protests and a deadly crackdown.

political_stability.notes: A 2024-25 switch to a parliamentary system made Gnassingbe President of the Council of Ministers - indefinitely renewable, an opposition-branded 'constitutional coup'; controlled but legitimacy-thin with succession-tail risk. (Al Jazeera; France 24; Africa Center)

civil_liberties.notes: Repressive - mid-2025 anti-government protests in Lome were met by a crackdown that killed at least five, with arrests and protest bans routine. (Africanews; Freedom House)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: No systematic abuse of foreigners; northern displacement and Sahel-refugee inflows are the main strain. (UNHCR; observed)

social_cohesion.notes: A long-standing north-south and ethnic cleavage overlaid on dynastic rule. (ICG/BTI)

demographics.notes: Very young and growing - a dividend with a jobs strain. (UN)

geopolitical.notes: A pragmatic ECOWAS/Western partner straddling the Sahel, with rising-but-contained northern jihadist spillover.

alliance_reliability.notes: An ECOWAS member and Western/EU partner, but pragmatically warm toward the Sahel juntas (AES) - a hedging posture. (observed; ICG)

conflict_involvement.notes: Rising JNIM-linked attacks in the northern Savanes region under a state of emergency - on an upward trend but casualties still below neighbors. (ACLED; ICG; Security Council Report)

physical_practical.notes: The strong Lome port anchors logistics, against coastal-erosion/flood exposure, a weak health system, and northern insecurity.

climate.notes: Coastal erosion on a very short low shore, northern drought, flooding, weak readiness. (ND-GAIN; WB)

healthcare.notes: Weak and underfunded, with low coverage. (World Bank/WHO)

infrastructure.notes: The Lome deep-water/transhipment port is a regional asset; inland roads, power, and the north lag. (AfDB; World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: Moderate in the south; the Savanes north carries jihadist and travel-advisory risk. (observed; OSAC)

summary: Togo pairs decent WAEMU macro performance with one of West Africa's most entrenched autocracies. The economy grows steadily (~5.3%), inflation is low and euro-peg-anchored, an IMF ECF is on track, and the Lome deep-water port plus phosphates give a regional logistics-and-mining base. The dominant story is political: the Gnassingbe family has ruled since 1967, and a 2024-25 constitutional rewrite to a parliamentary system made Faure Gnassingbe an indefinitely renewable President of the Council of Ministers - an opposition-branded 'constitutional coup' that triggered mid-2025 Lome protests and a crackdown killing at least five. Rising-but-still-contained JNIM spillover into the northern Savanes region, a squeezed press (RSF 121/180), coastal-erosion exposure, and a weak health system reinforce a negatively skewed profile: stable cash-flow fundamentals against poor governance, civil-liberties, and security tails.
